transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Before we start the usual standup — yesterday's demo did not go well. I want to understand what happened. Client saw the error screen during the payment flow. Their team is upset and BU head is now cc'd on all communication.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Sir, I'm sorry about that. What happened was — Varsha deployed the timeout fix and retry logic to staging-v2. Ajay ran the tests on staging-v2 and they passed. But the demo environment the client was looking at was staging-v1. The fix never reached that environment.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: I deployed to staging-v2 because that's what Rakesh told me in the war room. No one told me the client demo was on staging-v1.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: That's on me. I should have asked which environment the client was using before the demo. I assumed it was v2. My mistake.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Okay. I appreciate the honesty. But the damage is done. Now client wants the full payment flow working by Friday EOD. That is a hard commitment I made to them this morning. Not negotiable.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Understood sir. Ajay, what's the test status from last night? You ran on staging-v2?

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: Yes. 34 test cases total. 28 passing on staging-v2. 6 failing. Out of 6 — 4 are retry edge cases that Varsha hasn't finished yet, and 2 are mobile UI issues on the confirmation page.

transcript.speaker: Suraj
transcript.text: The 2 mobile issues — I know what they are. Ankit from my team is already on it. Should be done by lunch today.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Varsha — the 4 retry edge cases. Where are you on those?

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: 3 out of 4 are done. The 4th scenario is when the gateway itself is completely down — not slow, but completely unreachable. For that we need to save the transaction to DB, send user a notification, and retry asynchronously in the background. It's not a small change.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: How long for that 4th scenario?

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: Properly done — 1.5 to 2 days minimum. We're talking async job queue, DB schema change, notification service integration.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: We cannot do that by Friday. Vinod sir — we may need to descope the gateway-down scenario and document it as a known limitation. The other 3 retry cases cover 95% of real situations.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: I'll discuss with client. But Rupali — whatever we commit to Friday, those scenarios must be absolutely bulletproof. I cannot go back to them with another failure.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Understood sir. Ajay — can you send me the exact failing test case details? I'll sit with Varsha today and we'll divide and finish the 3 remaining retry cases.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: Sending in the channel right now.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Also — Vivek, today we deploy everything to both staging-v1 and staging-v2. No more confusion about which environment. Can you make sure both are in sync?

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: Yes. I'll set up a single pipeline that deploys to both. Will be done by noon.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Also everyone — please update JIRA today. Client's team has read access and they've been checking. Several tickets are still showing In Progress since Day 1. That doesn't look good.

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: Okay will update.

transcript.speaker: Suraj
transcript.text: Same.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Rupali — written status update to me by 6pm today. What's done, what's remaining, what's the Friday plan. I need to reply to BU head.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Will send by 6pm sir.
